skill.name: libreoffice-writer
skill.description: Use when creating, editing, formatting, or extracting LibreOffice Writer (.odt) documents via UNO, including text insertion, tables, images, metadata, and paragraph styling.
skill.summary: Automate LibreOffice Writer document operations via UNO bridge for creating, editing, formatting, and exporting ODT files.
skill.author: dfk1352

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 87 potential issues, but manual review confirms all are false positives. The skill is legitimate LibreOffice Writer automation using UNO bridge. Subprocess calls are for finding/launching LibreOffice, not user-controlled command execution. CSS color codes were misidentified as cryptographic hashes. Dynamic imports are standard UNO library loading patterns.

medium_findings.title: Subprocess Execution for LibreOffice Discovery
medium_findings.description: subprocess.run and subprocess.Popen are used in uno_bridge.py to find and launch LibreOffice. Arguments are hardcoded strings, not user input. No command injection risk exists.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Verified that subprocess arguments are hardcoded strings (soffice, libreoffice) and not user-controlled input.

content.value_statement: Automate creation, editing, and formatting of LibreOffice Writer documents directly from Claude or Codex. Generate reports, apply styling, insert images and tables, and export to PDF or DOCX.
actual_capabilities: Create new ODT documents with proper structure
Insert and format text with fonts, colors, alignment, and styles
Add tables with data and custom formatting
Insert images at specified positions with size control
Set and retrieve document metadata (title, author, keywords)
Export documents to PDF and DOCX formats
Capture visual snapshots of document pages for verification
limitations: Requires LibreOffice installed on the system
LibreOffice must be accessible in PATH or common installation locations
Only supports ODT format for native operations
Cannot edit existing non-ODT documents without conversion

use_cases.title: Generate automated reports
use_cases.description: Create quarterly reports, financial summaries, or status updates with consistent formatting, headers, and data tables.

use_cases.title: Batch process document templates
use_cases.description: Fill in template documents with dynamic data, apply branding, and export to final formats for distribution.

use_cases.title: Extract and process document content
use_cases.description: Read existing ODT documents to extract text, metadata, or convert content to other formats.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple document
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new LibreOffice Writer document at {output_path} with the title '{title}' and the following content: {content}
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to create a basic ODT file with text content

prompt_templates.title: Format document text
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply bold formatting with font size {size} to the last paragraph in {doc_path}. Make the paragraph center-aligned.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to apply specific formatting to document content

prompt_templates.title: Add table with data
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a table with {rows} rows and {cols} columns to {doc_path}. Populate it with the following data: {data}. Position it after paragraph {position}.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to insert tabular data into a document

prompt_templates.title: Export to PDF
prompt_templates.prompt: Export the document at {doc_path} to PDF format at {output_path}. Use the PDF export filter.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to convert ODT to PDF for distribution

output_examples.input: Create a new document at /tmp/report.odt with title 'Q1 Report' and content 'Sales: $50,000'
output_examples.output: Document created successfully at /tmp/report.odt with formatted title and content.

output_examples.input: Add a 3x2 table to /tmp/report.odt with headers [Metric, Value] and data [[Revenue, $50K], [Profit, $10K]]
output_examples.output: Table inserted at position 0 with headers and 2 data rows.
Document exported to PDF format successfully.

best_practices: Use absolute file paths for all document and image operations
Set PYTHONPATH to include the scripts directory before importing writer modules
Apply formatting after inserting text, using selection='last_paragraph' for the most recent content
anti_patterns: Using relative paths instead of absolute paths (UNO requires absolute URLs)
Forgetting to create the document before inserting content
Applying formatting before any text exists in the document

faq.question: Do I need LibreOffice installed to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill requires LibreOffice to be installed and accessible. It will auto-detect the installation path.

faq.question: What file formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill works with ODT files natively. Export to PDF and DOCX formats is supported.

faq.question: Can I edit existing Word documents?
faq.answer: Not directly. You would need to convert Word documents to ODT first, then use this skill.

faq.question: How do I set up the Python path correctly?
faq.answer: Set PYTHONPATH to include the scripts folder from this skill. For example: PYTHONPATH=/path/to/skill/scripts

faq.question: What does the snapshot feature do?
faq.answer: It captures a page of the document as a PNG image for visual verification of layout and formatting.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without a display?
faq.answer: Yes. LibreOffice runs in headless mode, so no display is required.
